artifact.content_preview:
MEETING NOTES — NGIS PHASE 3 STEERING COMMITTEE
Date: March 8, 2026 | Attendees: Director Okafor, Alice Tran, Bob Morrison, ADM Clarke

Item 5 (raised by ADM Clarke):

Following the recent cyber incident affecting a peer department's primary
data centre, ADM Clarke raised the requirement to add a dedicated disaster
recovery (DR) site to the NGIS Phase 3 scope.

ADM Clarke's request:
"Given the current threat environment and Treasury Board's updated guidance
on GC infrastructure resilience, I wou

extracted_tasks.text: Prepare DR site options analysis including identification of potential colocation facilities (e.g., Borden or Miramichi) and ensuring hot-standby replicas for all Phase 3 workloads.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Alice committed to conducting a preliminary scoping assessment and presenting options to the steering committee by March 15.

extracted_tasks.text: Submit formal change request for scope addition if cost impact exceeds 15% of approved budget.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Any formal scope addition would require a change request, ADM approval, and Treasury Board notification if the cost impact exceeds 15% of the approved

extracted_tasks.text: Obtain ADM approval for the new disaster recovery site scope addition.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Any formal scope addition would require a change request, ADM approval, and Treasury Board notification if the cost impact exceeds 15% of the approved

extracted_tasks.text: Notify Treasury Board regarding the scope addition.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: Any formal scope addition would require a change request, ADM approval, and Treasury Board notification if the cost impact exceeds 15% of the approved

extracted_tasks.text: Investigate adding a new disaster recovery site to NGIS Phase 3 scope as part of consolidation.
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: I would like the project team to investigate adding a new disaster recovery site as part of the Phase 3 consolidation.

metadata.date: March 8, 2026 | Attendees: Director Okafor, Alice Tran, Bob Morrison, ADM Clarke

alerts.detected_ask: Add a dedicated disaster recovery (DR) site with hot-standby replicas for all migrated workloads, involving identification of a new colocation facility (e.g., Borden or Miramichi).
alerts.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments; DELIVERABLES: No deliverable listed for DR site construction or hot-standby replication.
alerts.suggested_action: Formalize a Change Request (CR) to the Steering Committee, as this request falls outside the current Statement of Work and likely exceeds the 15% budget variance threshold requiring Treasury Board notification. llm_calls.content:
{
  "in_scope": [
    "Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure",
    "Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A",
    "Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A",
    "Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A",
    "Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites",
    "Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones",
    "BGP routing table migration and validation",
    "Network performance baseline testing post-migration",
    "Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A",
    "AD schema extension and forest restructuring",
    "Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing",
    "Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD",
    "Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office",
    "Steering committee presentations (monthly)",
    "Risk register maintenance",
    "Change management and stakeholder communications"
  ],
  "out_of_scope": [
    "End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices)",
    "Application modernization or re-platforming (changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform)",
    "GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud)",
    "End-user training and change management beyond project communications",
    "Procurement of new application licenses",
    "Network monitoring tool upgrades"
  ],
  "deliverables": [
    "Project Management Plan (Due: October 31, 2024)",
    "Technical Design Document (TDD) (Due: December 15, 2024)",
    "Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package (Due: January 31, 2025)",
    "Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) (Due: February 28, 2025)",
    "User Acceptance Test (UAT) Plan (Due: March 10, 2026)",
    "Site A Consolidation Complete (Go-Live) (Due: April 30, 2026)",
    "Post-Migration Validation Report (Due: May 31, 2026)",
    "Project Closure Report (Due: June 30, 2026)"
  ],
  "constraints": [
    "Total approved budget cap of $2,400,000 inclusive of all fees and taxes",
    "Budget variance exceeding 15% requires formal amendment and Treasury Board submission",
    "Go-Live deadline no later than April 30, 2026",
    "Final project closure date June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year 2025–2026)",
    "Extensions beyond closure date require ADM-level approval",
    "Compliance with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management",
    "Compliance with Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy",
    "Compliance with Treasury Board Policy on Government Security",
    "Compliance with Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)",
    "Compliance with Official Languages Act for client-facing deliverables",
    "All scope changes require written approval from Project Sponsor and impact assessment"
  ]
}

in_scope: Decommissioning of Sites B and C physical infrastructure
Migration of all server workloads from Sites B and C to Site A
Physical server rack consolidation and cable management at Site A
Environmental systems (HVAC, UPS, fire suppression) optimization at Site A
Replacement and deployment of Cisco Catalyst 9500 series switches across all three sites
Network segmentation reconfiguration per updated security zones
BGP routing table migration and validation
Migration of all on-premise Active Directory domain controllers to consolidated infrastructure at Site A
AD schema extension and forest restructuring
Group Policy Object (GPO) consolidation and testing
Integration testing with downstream applications dependent on AD
Weekly status reports to SSC Project Management Office
out_of_scope: End-user device refresh or replacement (laptops, desktops, mobile devices)
Application modernization or re-platforming (changes to application code, architecture, or hosting platform)
GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments (AWS, Azure, GC-Cloud)
End-user training and change management beyond project communications
deliverables: Security Assessment and Authorization (SA&A) Package (Due: January 31, 2025)
Migration Runbook (Sites B and C) (Due: February 28, 2025)
constraints: Total approved budget cap of $2,400,000 inclusive of all fees and taxes
Budget variance exceeding 15% requires formal amendment and Treasury Board submission
Go-Live deadline no later than April 30, 2026
Final project closure date June 30, 2026 (end of fiscal year 2025–2026)
Extensions beyond closure date require ADM-level approval
Compliance with ITSG-33: IT Security Risk Management
Compliance with Government of Canada Cloud Adoption Strategy
Compliance with Treasury Board Policy on Government Security
Compliance with Privacy Act (R.S.C., 1985, c. P-21)
Compliance with Official Languages Act for client-facing deliverables
All scope changes require written approval from Project Sponsor and impact assessment

llm_calls.content:
{
  "alignment_score": 0.2,
  "drift_type": "new_requirement",
  "detected_ask": "Add a dedicated disaster recovery (DR) site with hot-standby replicas for all migrated workloads, involving identification of a new colocation facility (e.g., Borden or Miramichi).",
  "baseline_reference": "OUT OF SCOPE: GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments; DELIVERABLES: No deliverable listed for DR site construction or hot-standby replication.",
  "suggested_action": "Formalize a Change Request (CR) to the Steering Committee, as this request falls outside the current Statement of Work and likely exceeds the 15% budget variance threshold requiring Treasury Board notification. Conduct a cost-benefit analysis comparing the DR site against the existing $2.4M cap before proceeding.",
  "reasoning": "The document explicitly requests the creation of a new physical infrastructure component (DR site) with specific technical requirements (hot-standby replication) that are not present in the approved scope baseline. While the project is currently within the 'migration' phase, adding a secondary active/standby site constitutes a fundamental expansion of the architectural scope and deliverables, distinct from the defined consolidation to Site A only."
}

parsed_json.detected_ask: Add a dedicated disaster recovery (DR) site with hot-standby replicas for all migrated workloads, involving identification of a new colocation facility (e.g., Borden or Miramichi).
parsed_json.baseline_reference: OUT OF SCOPE: GC-Cloud workload migration to public cloud environments; DELIVERABLES: No deliverable listed for DR site construction or hot-standby replication.
parsed_json.suggested_action: Formalize a Change Request (CR) to the Steering Committee, as this request falls outside the current Statement of Work and likely exceeds the 15% budget variance threshold requiring Treasury Board notification. Conduct a cost-benefit analysis comparing the DR site against the existing $2.4M cap before proceeding.
parsed_json.reasoning: The document explicitly requests the creation of a new physical infrastructure component (DR site) with specific technical requirements (hot-standby replication) that are not present in the approved scope baseline. While the project is currently within the 'migration' phase, adding a secondary active/standby site constitutes a fundamental expansion of the architectural scope and deliverables, distinct from the defined consolidation to Site A only.
